paper_evidence.summary: RAS mutations (K- or N-RAS at codons 12, 13, 61) are significantly more frequent in MM (31%) vs MGUS (5%), suggesting a role in malignant transformation.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: MM: 18/58 (31%) RAS+; MGUS: 1/20 (5%) RAS+; p-value not provided

alignment.notes: Ground truth over-specifies both variant details (specific G12 mutations) and therapy (Melphalan) that are not discussed in paper. Paper focuses on RAS mutation frequency differences between disease states rather than therapy prediction.

missing_from_ai.reason_missing: AI extraction focused on prognostic/molecular associations rather than therapy predictions. The paper does not actually discuss melphalan response.

ai_improvements_over_gt.paper_support: Results section shows RAS mutations in 3/6 extramedullary but 0/13 intramedullary samples from ExPCT patients
Results show significant association between RAS mutations and cyclin D1 expression (P=0.015)
Results show reduced RAS mutation frequency in t(4;14) subset (P=0.055)

overall_judgement.summary: The AI extraction captures important molecular/prognostic associations from the paper that are missing from the ground truth, while avoiding over-specification of variants and therapies. However, it misses the ground truth's therapy prediction angle, though this appears to be over-specified beyond the paper's actual findings.
strengths: Accurately captures multiple molecular associations (RAS with MGUS/MM status, extramedullary disease, cyclin D1, t(4;14))
Maintains appropriate level of variant specification (mutation vs specific amino acid changes)
Includes quantitative support and statistical significance
weaknesses: Does not capture the therapy prediction aspect present in ground truth (though this may be appropriate given paper content)
Some evidence levels and significance claims are overstated relative to observational nature of data
Misses potentially important CD56 expression association with disease progression
